mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering negative risk sentiment and downward S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 70% of observations during confirmed expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index levels trigger immediate market repricing for tighter monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels show zero or positive correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Non-farm payrolls growth falling below 100,000 signals weakening labor demand, which reduces consumer spending forecasts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of months where PAYEMS growth drops below 100,000.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales indicates underlying demand resilience that markets underprice, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index signals immediate supply-side contraction, prompting equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO MoM declines coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of observations across all tested regimes.
